memo_text:
CATEGORY: CRM
CURRENT TOOL: NexusCRM (£510/mo)
COMPETITOR: VelocityCRM (£389.06/mo at 22 seats, Core tier)
DATE: 2024-11-10

PUSH SIGNALS:
  - No native LinkedIn enrichment — reps manually copy contact data [LOW — operational friction]
  - Email sequence builder limited to 5 steps [MEDIUM — competitor standard is unlimited]
  - Mobile app crashes on iOS 17+ [MEDIUM — reported by field sales team]

PULL SIGNALS:
  - Price reduction: £510 → £389.06/mo at 22 seats (Core tier) [POSITIVE — £1,452/yr direct saving]
  - HARD BLOCK: No SSO on Core tier [BLOCKER — global.md requires SSO for tools with >10 seats; Meridian has 22 CRM seats]
  - HARD BLOCK: No audit log on Core tier [BLOCKER — global.md requires exportable audit log for CRM]
  - "SSO and audit logging are available on Advanced tier at £28/seat" — Advanced tier £616/mo exceeds current spend [NEGATIVE — eliminates saving]
  - SOC2 Type II: not certified on Core tier [BLOCKER — mandatory per global.md, no exceptions for client-data tools]

FINANCIAL ANALYSIS:
  Migration cost: 8hrs × £48 = £384 one-time
  Annual saving: £1,452 (Core tier)
  Amortised migration: £128/yr over 3 years
  Annual net: £1,324
  ROI threshold (£1,200/yr): MET on direct saving (Core tier)
  Note: ROI calculation is moot — hard compliance blocks prevent Core tier adoption. Advanced tier (£616/mo) exceeds current spend; ROI would be negative.
  ROI threshold met: NO (compliance blocks supersede financial analysis)

VERDICT: STAY

EVIDENCE:
  "SSO and audit logging are available on Advanced tier at £28/seat"
  "VelocityCRM Core is designed for growing sales teams that need a clean, fast CRM without the overhead of enterprise feature bloat."
